text:
Once upon a time in a quiet little town, there was a gentle cow named Daisy. Daisy loved rainbows—especially those that stretched across twilight sky. She liked to look at them all night long.

One particular evening, after a storm, Daisy saw a beautiful rainbow arch so wide it seemed to touch the clouds themselves. She thought, "That would be wonderful."

So she followed it. It swayed and curled upwards. Daisy crooned soft melodies. Birds joined in. Dawn broke, and the rainbow faded. But Daisy remained.

She became famous. Birds sang her songs. People came far and wide. They said her songs could soothe wolves. So she sang some more.

One day an big bad wolf really did want to sing. He crooned loudly. People listened. They laughed.

"Daisy, you little hero!"

That night, big wolf got sleepy.

"Sleep well," Daisy crooned softly.

And he did. Maybe she saved more than birds.

Years passed. Daisy
